description: Google Maps accommodation scraper with interactive Explorer UI — see SPEC.md for full specification, DEVELOPMENT.md for implementation guide

userStories.title: Project restructure — monorepo setup
userStories.description: Set up monorepo with server/ and client/ packages. Move existing MVP to legacy/. Server: Express + TypeScript. Client: Vite + React + TypeScript. Root npm workspaces. .env.example with GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY.
acceptanceCriteria: npm install from root succeeds
server/ and client/ each have package.json and tsconfig.json
legacy/src/index.ts contains the original MVP code
npm run typecheck passes in both server/ and client/
npm run build succeeds in both packages
server starts and responds to GET /health with 200
client dev server starts and renders a placeholder page
.env.example exists with GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY placeholder

userStories.title: SQLite database schema and data access layer
userStories.description: Create SQLite schema covering all entities from SPEC.md §6: Project, ScrapeRun, Tile, Place, Review, PlaceScrapeRun, Shortlist, ShortlistEntry. Use better-sqlite3. Typed query functions for CRUD. Tests for schema creation and basic CRUD.
acceptanceCriteria: better-sqlite3 installed in server/
Schema creates all tables: projects, scrape_runs, tiles, places, reviews, place_scrape_runs, shortlists, shortlist_entries
Typed CRUD functions exist for each entity
Tests pass: create DB, insert/read/update/delete for Project and Place entities
npm run typecheck passes in server/
Database file is created in a configurable location (default: data/gomaps.db)

userStories.title: Express API skeleton with project CRUD
userStories.description: Express server with route modules for projects, scrapes, places, shortlists. Full CRUD for Projects endpoint. Other routes export placeholder routers. Integrate with SQLite DB layer from US-002.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/projects returns list of projects
POST /api/projects creates a project (name, bounds)
GET /api/projects/:id returns single project
PUT /api/projects/:id updates project
DELETE /api/projects/:id deletes project
Placeholder routers mounted at /api/scrape, /api/places, /api/shortlists
npm run typecheck passes
Integration tests pass for project CRUD

userStories.title: React app shell with Google Maps
userStories.description: Bootstrap React app with Vite. React Router with pages: Setup, Explorer, Shortlists. Embed Google Maps on Setup page using @vis.gl/react-google-maps. Map renders, is interactive. Proxy API requests to Express server.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run dev starts the React dev server
React Router navigates between Setup, Explorer, Shortlists pages
Google Maps renders on Setup page (interactive — pan, zoom)
API key loaded from VITE_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY env var
Vite proxy forwards /api/* requests to Express server
npm run typecheck passes in client/
npm run build succeeds in client/

userStories.title: Extract scraper into modular engine
userStories.description: Refactor MVP scraper logic from legacy/src/index.ts into server/src/scraper/engine.ts. Importable module (not standalone script). Accepts config via parameters. Writes results to SQLite instead of JSON/CSV. Preserves: URL collection, detail extraction, CAPTCHA pause, checkpoint/resume.
acceptanceCriteria: server/src/scraper/engine.ts exports a startScrape() function
Function accepts typed config: query, bounds, browser options
Scraped places are inserted into SQLite Place table
Reviews are inserted into SQLite Review table
CAPTCHA detection and pause mechanism preserved
URL normalization and lat/lng parsing extracted as testable functions
Unit tests pass for URL normalization, lat/lng parsing
npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Adaptive tiling algorithm
userStories.description: Implement server/src/scraper/tiling.ts. Given bounding box, generate coarse grid (~0.1°). Process tiles one at a time. If result count >= threshold (~120), subdivide into 4 sub-tiles. Track tile status in DB. Enforce minimum tile size floor. See SPEC.md §5.
acceptanceCriteria: generateTiles(bounds) returns initial grid of tile objects
subdivideTile(tile) returns 4 child tiles
shouldSubdivide(resultCount, threshold) returns boolean
Minimum tile size floor (0.01°) prevents infinite recursion
Tile status tracked in SQLite: pending, running, completed, subdivided
Progress can be queried: tiles total/completed/subdivided, places found
Unit tests pass for tile generation, subdivision, bounds math
npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Enhanced detail extraction
userStories.description: Extend scraper to extract: priceLevel, photoUrls (first N), openingHours (text), amenities (best-effort from About tab). Store reviews in Review table with individual rating + text. See SPEC.md §7 extraction table.
acceptanceCriteria: Place records include priceLevel (nullable string)
Place records include photoUrls (array of URLs)
Place records include openingHours (nullable string)
Place records include amenities (array of strings)
Reviews stored in reviews table with placeId, rating, text, relativeDate
Extraction handles missing fields gracefully (nulls, empty arrays)
npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Website classification
userStories.description: Implement server/src/scraper/classifier.ts. Classify place website domain as direct/ota/social/unknown. Hardcoded allowlist of ~30 OTA and social domains. See SPEC.md §7 classification table.
acceptanceCriteria: classifyWebsite(url) returns 'direct' | 'ota' | 'social' | 'unknown'
Correctly classifies: booking.com, airbnb.com, expedia.com, hotels.com, vrbo.com, agoda.com, tripadvisor.com → ota
Correctly classifies: facebook.com, instagram.com → social
Correctly classifies: www.hotel-example.it → direct
Handles subdomains: www.booking.com → ota
Handles null/undefined input → unknown
Place records include websiteType field populated by classifier
Comprehensive tests pass (15+ test cases)
npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Scrape API — start, monitor, pause with SSE progress
userStories.description: Wire scraper into Express API. POST to start scrape (projectId + query). GET scrape run status. POST to pause/resume. SSE endpoint for live progress. Scraper runs as background async task in server process.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/scrape/start accepts { projectId, query } and returns scrapeRunId
GET /api/scrape/:runId returns run status, tile progress, place counts
POST /api/scrape/:runId/pause pauses the running scrape
POST /api/scrape/:runId/resume resumes a paused scrape
GET /api/scrape/:runId/progress is an SSE endpoint streaming progress events
Progress events include: { tilesCompleted, tilesTotal, tilesSubdivided, placesFound, placesUnique, elapsedMs }
Scrape runs in background — API remains responsive during scrape
npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Project management UI
userStories.description: React Projects page. List projects (name, date, place count). New Project button → form. Clicking project navigates to its Setup page. Fetch from /api/projects.
acceptanceCriteria: Projects page lists all projects from API
Each project shows name, creation date, place count
'New Project' button opens a creation form (name input)
Creating a project calls POST /api/projects and refreshes list
Clicking a project navigates to /projects/:id/setup
Delete button removes project (with confirmation)
npm run typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds

userStories.title: Google Maps area selection with drawable rectangle
userStories.description: On project Setup page, Google Maps with 'Select Area' button. Current viewport becomes a draggable/resizable rectangle. Bounding box coords displayed and saved to project via API. Existing bounds shown on load.
acceptanceCriteria: Setup page shows full Google Maps view for the project
'Select Area' button creates a rectangle matching current viewport
Rectangle is draggable and resizable with handles
Bounding box coordinates (SW lat/lng, NE lat/lng) displayed below map
'Save Area' persists bounds to project via PUT /api/projects/:id
On page load, existing project bounds are shown as rectangle on map
npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Scrape launch and live progress display
userStories.description: Query input + Start Scrape button on Setup page. Start calls API. Progress panel subscribes to SSE. Shows live stats: tiles, places, elapsed, ETA. Tile status overlay on map (green/yellow/gray rectangles).
acceptanceCriteria: Query text input field on Setup page
'Start Scrape' button calls POST /api/scrape/start
Progress panel appears showing live stats from SSE
Stats shown: tiles completed/total, places found (unique), elapsed time, estimated remaining
Tile rectangles overlaid on Google Maps: green=completed, yellow=running, gray=pending
Pause/Resume button toggles scrape state
User can navigate away and return to see current progress
npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Map view with place markers and clustering
userStories.description: Explorer page. Fetch places for current project. Render as custom markers on Google Maps. Color by rating (red/yellow/green). Marker clustering when zoomed out. Click marker to select.
acceptanceCriteria: Explorer page at /projects/:id/explorer
GET /api/places?projectId=X returns all places for project
Places rendered as colored markers on Google Maps
Marker color by rating: red (< 3.5), yellow (3.5–4.2), green (> 4.2)
Marker clustering via @googlemaps/markerclusterer when zoomed out
Clicking a marker selects it (visually distinct — larger or highlighted)
npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Place table with sorting and virtual scrolling
userStories.description: Table below map on Explorer page. Columns: Name, Category, Rating, Reviews, Price, Website (linked), Address. Sortable columns. Virtual scrolling for 5000+ rows. Inline text filter.
acceptanceCriteria: Table displays all places for the current project
Columns: Name, Category, Rating, Review Count, Price Level, Website, Address
Clicking column header sorts ascending/descending
Website column renders as clickable link
Virtual scrolling handles 5000+ rows without lag
Inline text filter above table filters rows across all text columns
npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Detail panel with reviews
userStories.description: Right-side panel showing selected place details. All fields from SPEC.md §8.4: name, rating, price, address, phone, website with Direct/OTA badge, amenities, photos, hours, reviews. Action link: Open in Google Maps.
acceptanceCriteria: Panel opens on right side when a place is selected
Displays all place fields: name, category, rating, reviewCount, priceLevel, address, phone, website, amenities, photoUrls, openingHours, scrapedAt
Phone rendered as clickable tel: link
Website shows badge: green 'Book Direct' for direct, orange 'OTA' for ota, gray for none
Photo URLs rendered as clickable thumbnail strip
Reviews section shows all reviews (rating + text)
Reviews searchable via text input within panel
'Open in Google Maps' link opens googleUrl in new tab
npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Map ↔ Table bidirectional selection sync
userStories.description: Clicking map marker highlights table row + opens detail panel. Clicking table row centers map on marker + opens detail panel. Selected marker has distinct visual style.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking a map marker: scrolls table to corresponding row, highlights it, opens detail panel
Clicking a table row: centers map on corresponding marker, opens detail panel
Selected marker has distinct visual style (larger/different color/bouncing)
Only one place selected at a time
Deselecting (clicking empty area) closes detail panel
npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fuzzy text search
userStories.description: Search bar at top of Explorer. Fuzzy search across place name, address, category, amenities, review text. Updates map (only matching markers) and table. Use Fuse.js or SQLite FTS5. Debounce 300ms.
acceptanceCriteria: Search input at top of Explorer page
Typing filters places by fuzzy match across: name, address, category, amenities, review text
Map shows only matching markers
Table shows only matching rows
Search is debounced (300ms)
Clearing search restores all results
npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Filter panel
userStories.description: Collapsible filter panel on Explorer. Filters: rating range slider, min review count, category multi-select, has website toggle, website type (direct/OTA), price level multi-select. AND logic. Updates map + table live. Persists in URL params.
acceptanceCriteria: Collapsible/expandable filter panel on Explorer page
Rating: range slider (1.0–5.0)
Min reviews: number input
Category: multi-select checkboxes (populated from data)
Has website: toggle (Yes/No/Any)
Website type: checkboxes (Direct/OTA/Social)
Price level: multi-select ($, $$, $$$, $$$$)
All filters combine with AND logic
Map markers and table update live as filters change
Filter state persisted in URL query parameters
npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Review keyword search and distance filter
userStories.description: Two advanced filters: (1) Review keyword text input — filter to places with matching reviews, highlight keyword in detail panel. (2) Distance from point — click map to set center point, radius slider (1–50km), filter places within radius.
acceptanceCriteria: Review keyword filter: text input that filters to places where >= 1 review contains the keyword
Matching keywords highlighted in detail panel review section
Distance filter: click map to set center point (shown as marker)
Radius slider (1–50km) appears after point is set
Only places within radius are shown on map and table
Circle overlay drawn on map showing the radius
Both filters integrate with existing filter system (AND logic)
npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Star/bookmark places and shortlist management
userStories.description: Star toggle in table, map popups, detail panel. Stars saved to default shortlist via API. Shortlists page: list/create/rename/delete shortlists. Move places between shortlists. Per-entry notes. Full shortlist CRUD API.
acceptanceCriteria: ⭐ toggle button in table rows, detail panel
Starring a place saves it to default shortlist via API
POST/GET/PUT/DELETE /api/shortlists endpoints work
POST/DELETE /api/shortlists/:id/entries endpoints work
Shortlists page lists all shortlists for a project
Can create, rename, delete shortlists
Can add free-text notes to each shortlist entry
Can move entries between shortlists
npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Comparison view and CSV export
userStories.description: On Shortlists page, select 2–3 places for side-by-side comparison. Comparison table shows key fields in columns. Export CSV button downloads shortlist as CSV with all place fields + user notes.
acceptanceCriteria: Checkbox selection on shortlist entries (max 3)
'Compare' button opens side-by-side comparison view
Comparison shows: name, rating, reviews, price, website, amenities, address
'Export CSV' button downloads shortlist as CSV
CSV includes all place fields plus user notes
npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Re-scrape support
userStories.description: Refresh Data button on project page. Re-visits detail pages of all existing places to update data. Skips tiling/discovery. Special ScrapeRun type. Progress shown in UI.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Refresh Data' button on project Setup page
POST /api/scrape/rescrape accepts { projectId } and creates a re-scrape run
Re-scrape iterates existing place URLs, not tiles
Updated place data (rating, reviews, price, etc.) overwrites old data
scrapedAt timestamp updated for each re-scraped place
Progress shown via SSE (places refreshed / total)
npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: External search links (Booking.com, Airbnb)
userStories.description: In detail panel, add auto-generated 'Search on Booking.com' and 'Search on Airbnb' links. Encode place name + location into OTA search URLs. Open in new tab.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Search on Booking.com' link in detail panel
'Search on Airbnb' link in detail panel
Links encode place name and location into OTA search URL format
Links open in new tab (target=_blank)
Links only shown when place has a name and location
npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Stale data indicators
userStories.description: Visual indicator in table and detail panel for places with scrapedAt > 7 days old. Configurable threshold. 'Show stale only' toggle in filter panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Places with scrapedAt older than threshold show stale indicator (icon + tooltip) in table
Stale indicator also shown in detail panel
Threshold configurable (default: 7 days)
'Show stale only' toggle added to filter panel
Stale filter integrates with existing filter system
npm run typecheck passes
